rationale: Both abstracts are strong and nearly equivalent. Each defines a specific, testable ZIP-code-level utilization gap and reports effect sizes with exposure increments and P values, although neither provides confidence intervals. Y is slightly clearer and more economical in the Objective, Methods, and Results, with cleaner phrasing around quantifying associations. Both conclusions somewhat overstate an ecological observational design by using inequity language as a finding rather than limiting the claim to lower utilization associated with area composition.
loser_weakness: X most lacked the tighter phrasing and economy of Y; its conclusion is also slightly less calibrated because it states that inequities exist rather than emphasizing observed utilization associations.
